Onychomycosis (Tinea unguium) is a superficial, non-lethal fungal infection of the nails. Fungal vaccines are notoriously difficult to develop due to the thick fungal cell wall and similarities between fungal and human cells. Currently, there are no approved human fungal vaccines for any disease. 

A thorough search of ongoing clinical trials and drug pipelines for onychomycosis (involving companies like Moberg Pharma, Blueberry Therapeutics, and Hallux) indicates that current R&D is exclusively focused on oral therapies, topical drugs (like terbinafine), and new delivery mechanisms (like nanoformulations). There are absolutely no active clinical trials or preclinical human candidates for an onychomycosis vaccine. Even an emerging bioinformatics study suggesting mRNA vaccines for dermatophytosis is completely theoretical at this stage. 

Because onychomycosis is non-lethal, highly treatable with existing antifungals, and localized to avascular tissues, there is extremely low commercial or clinical incentive to develop a preventative or therapeutic vaccine. Given the total absence of early-stage candidates and the typical 10-15 year development timeline for novel human vaccines, the probability of one being developed and added to the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page by 2036 remains incredibly small. Probabilities are set to the minimum constrained bound for the next several years, with very small increases for the 2031 and 2036 horizons to account for fundamental long-term uncertainty.

A thorough search of ongoing clinical trials and drug pipelines for onychomycosis (involving companies like Moberg Pharma, Blueberry Therapeutics, and Hallux) indicates that current R&D is exclusively focused on oral therapies, topical drugs (like terbinafine), and new delivery mechanisms (like nanoformulations). There are absolutely no active clinical trials or preclinical human candidates for an onychomycosis vaccine. Even an emerging bioinformatics study suggesting mRNA vaccines for dermatophytosis is completely theoretical at this stage. 

Because onychomycosis is non-lethal, highly treatable with existing antifungals, and localized to avascular tissues, there is extremely low commercial or clinical incentive to develop a preventative or therapeutic vaccine. Given the total absence of early-stage candidates and the typical 10-15 year development timeline for novel human vaccines, the probability of one being developed and added to the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page by 2036 remains incredibly small. Probabilities are set to the minimum constrained bound for the next several years, with very small increases for the 2031 and 2036 horizons to account for fundamental long-term uncertainty.

The base rate for a top 20 player gaining 1% Elo (+28 points for Giri) is very low (~0.007). Anish Giri's Elo as of April 2026 is 2753, requiring him to reach at least 2781 to resolve YES. He is currently playing in the Candidates 2026 and lost in Round 1, so his live rating has dropped. Given the K-factor of 10 for super-GMs, gaining 28 points requires an anomalous winning streak (+2.8 wins over expected score) within a short window, which is extremely unlikely by late 2026. Therefore, short-term probabilities (2026) are floored at 0.05. Over the medium-to-long term (2027-2036), probabilities progressively increase to account for potential FIDE rating inflation, shifts in rating calculation methodology, or unlikely late-career surges, though his age (31 in 2026, 41 in 2036) and primarily solid playstyle keep the overall chances reasonably low.

Alina Kashlinskaya is currently ranked precisely 15th on the April 2026 rating list, meaning her rank meets the 'as high or higher' (<= 15) threshold. Revisions to FIDE ratings occur monthly on the 1st. For the first resolution date (2026-04-19), the April list will still be actively displayed, guaranteeing a YES (represented by an extremely high probability of 0.98). In subsequent months, she is just on the cusp of rank 15. The player directly behind her (Tsolakidou) is only 1 point away, and Vaishali is competing in the active Candidates tournament which could drastically shift her own rating. While short horizons maintain a moderate chance that she holds onto her rank or climbs due to above her falling, longer horizons carry stronger demographic and competitive headwinds. As a 32-year-old, her chances of maintaining a top 15 spot against quickly rising junior players (e.g. Divya Deshmukh, Carissa Yip, Zhu Jiner) diminish in her late 30s and 40s. Thus, expectations gradually fall below 50% over the next decade, while being kept reasonably conservative (approaching 0.5) to account for increasing baseline uncertainty over far-distant dates.

Currently on 2026-04-12, Vincent Keymer is ranked World No. 5 with an Elo of 2762. The resolution dates ask the probability of his rank being 5 or higher. 

For the nearest date, 2026-04-19 (in 7 days), the FIDE rankings on Wikipedia will almost certainly still reflect the official April 1 list. Thus, the probability is extremely high (~0.96) that his rank will remain 5.

For 2026-05-12, the ratings will reflect the May 1 FIDE list, which will include results from the Candidates Tournament ending in April. Keymer is not playing in the Candidates, but his immediate chasers (Wei Yi at #8 with 2754, and Anish Giri at #9 with 2753) are competing. Furthermore, Alireza Firouzja at #6 (2759) is only 3 rating points behind Keymer and actively plays. Due to the high-stakes games in April where lower-rated top players can harvest points from Caruana and Nakamura, there's a tangible risk (approx. 35%) that one of them overtakes Keymer, warranting a drop to 0.65.

For later dates, the volatility of chess rankings requires forecasting his long-term steady-state ranking. Keymer is 21 years old and considered a generational talent. Given that many of the current top-5 players (Carlsen, Nakamura, Caruana) are in their 30s and likely to slip in classical rankings over the next decade, Keymer is well-positioned to remain in, or frequently rotate into, the top 5 during his chess prime (he will be 31 in 2036). His chances decrease smoothly over time due to accumulating variance and emerging competitors, leveling out around a ~50% probability that he is holding a top 5 spot at any arbitrary moment in 5-10 years.

For the nearest date, 2026-04-19 (in 7 days), the FIDE rankings on Wikipedia will almost certainly still reflect the official April 1 list. Thus, the probability is extremely high (~0.96) that his rank will remain 5.

For 2026-05-12, the ratings will reflect the May 1 FIDE list, which will include results from the Candidates Tournament ending in April. Keymer is not playing in the Candidates, but his immediate chasers (Wei Yi at #8 with 2754, and Anish Giri at #9 with 2753) are competing. Furthermore, Alireza Firouzja at #6 (2759) is only 3 rating points behind Keymer and actively plays. Due to the high-stakes games in April where lower-rated top players can harvest points from Caruana and Nakamura, there's a tangible risk (approx. 35%) that one of them overtakes Keymer, warranting a drop to 0.65.

For later dates, the volatility of chess rankings requires forecasting his long-term steady-state ranking. Keymer is 21 years old and considered a generational talent. Given that many of the current top-5 players (Carlsen, Nakamura, Caruana) are in their 30s and likely to slip in classical rankings over the next decade, Keymer is well-positioned to remain in, or frequently rotate into, the top 5 during his chess prime (he will be 31 in 2036). His chances decrease smoothly over time due to accumulating variance and emerging competitors, leveling out around a ~50% probability that he is holding a top 5 spot at any arbitrary moment in 5-10 years.

Wesley So is ranked exactly #7 on the April 2026 FIDE ranking list (2754 Elo). "As high or higher" means he must maintain rank #1 through #7. 
1. April 19, 2026: FIDE lists update strictly monthly on the first of the month. Wikipedia closely aligns with this list. Therefore, on April 19, his rank will remain at exactly 7, guaranteeing a YES result except in extreme anomalies (giving ~97%).
2. May 12, 2026: By this date, the new May FIDE rating list will be reflected. He is intensely clustered among competitors: tied exactly at 2754 with Wei Yi (#8), and chased by Giri (2753), Erigaisi (2751), and Sindarov (2745). Critically, Wesley is not competing in the April 2026 Candidates Tournament, but several immediate peers (e.g., Sindarov, who started exceptionally hot at 2.5/3) are currently gaining rating points. Because Wesley's rating remains stagnant, the probability he drops to #8 or lower purely through the volatile gains of Candidates players is roughly a coin flip, yielding ~48%.
3. Long-term (2026 to 2036): Wesley is currently 32 years old. Historically, maintaining a top-7 presence throughout one's mid-30s into the early 40s (he will be 42 in 2036) is exceedingly difficult, especially facing the massive influx of 2750+ rated prodigies (Praggnanandhaa, Sindarov, Gukesh, Abdusattorov, Keymer, Firouzja). I model a steadily decaying probability as his aging curve sets in against these younger peers. To account for structural uncertainties over wide time horizons, I keep distant probabilities constrained towards 0.5 rather than projecting absolute zero.

As of April 2026, the Wikipedia page for the list of infectious diseases lists 'No' under the vaccine column for Chagas disease. A web search reveals that research into a vaccine for Chagas disease is still in the preclinical or very early Phase 1 stages (e.g., testing recombinant antigens in animal models). The development of a vaccine for a complex parasitic disease such as Chagas is notoriously difficult, and usually takes 10 to 15 years from early clinical stages to approval, if successful at all. Therefore, the probability of a vaccine being developed and listed on Wikipedia is practically zero (0.02, the minimum allowed value) in the short term (2026-2027), and remains very low even for a horizon of 3-5 years (2029-2031), gradually increasing for the 10-year horizon (2036) to account for increasing uncertainty and the possibility that current preclinical candidates succeed.

The probability relies on two main factors: the development of a scientifically recognized 'vaccine' for Chytridiomycosis (which is currently a highly active area of research, with experimental 'transmissible' and metabolic-product-based vaccines showing promise as of 2023-2026), and the subsequent update of the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia page to reflect this. Currently, the table row for Chytridiomycosis has a blank entry for the 'Vaccine(s)' column, which is treated as 'No'. Over the long term (5-10 years), the chances of these experimental therapies gaining enough traction to be listed on this Wikipedia page increase substantially as amphibian conservation efforts scale. In the short term, the chances of rapid editing or official approval of a new vaccine remain very low but non-zero, especially given recent media coverage of 'transmissible vaccines' in Feb 2026. Therefore, probabilities follow an increasing hazard model, growing from around 2% for immediate dates to 35% for ten years out.

Cameron McEvoy set a remarkable new world record of 20.88 in the 50m freestyle on March 20, 2026, at the China Open. This finally breaks Cesar Cielo's 2009 super-suit-era record of 20.91. The event is official, and McEvoy has publicly spoken against doping (criticizing the 'Enhanced Games' for paying dopers while real WRs get nothing), strongly suggesting this was a clean swim. Ratification risk is very low (likely under 1-2%). 

In the short run (1 week to 1 year), it is extremely unlikely that the record will be broken by someone else, as McEvoy shattered a historic barrier by a significant margin (0.03s under the supersuit record and 0.16s below the previous best textile time). I've capped the initial probabilities at the 0.95 limit to respect the rule constraint. Over the longer term, the probability of him remaining the sole record holder gradually decreases, especially dropping after the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics and the 2032 Brisbane Olympics, where swimming technology and younger competitors might finally catch up. However, supersuit era records showed us that the 50m freestyle is a very difficult event to progress in, so even by 2036, there is still a noticeable probability (about 42%) that McEvoy's 20.88 mark — or an even faster time he sets before retiring — still stands as the world record.

Parasite vaccines are notoriously difficult to develop and generally take decades (e.g., the malaria vaccine took over 30 years). Amoebiasis is a neglected tropical disease with low funding for research. Searches indicate that as of 2021, and continuing to the present day, no vaccine candidates for Entamoeba histolytica have even progressed to human clinical testing (Phase I). Since the standard timeline for clinical testing (Phases I, II, and III) and regulatory approval is highly unlikely to be completed within 5-10 years — especially starting from a pre-clinical stage for a complex protozoan parasite — the probability of a vaccine being developed and listed on Wikipedia remains extremely low. Given the forecasting constraints, probabilities are set to the minimum 5% for the short-to-medium horizons (2026-2027), increasing slightly over the 3- to 10-year horizons (to 12% by 2036) to account for scientific breakthroughs or accelerated development pathways.

The question asks whether Wikipedia will list a vaccine for Paracoccidioidomycosis as having been developed by various resolution dates between 2026 and 2036. Paracoccidioidomycosis is a fungal infection endemic to Latin America. A search of recent scientific literature and medical outlines confirms that there is currently no vaccine available for this disease. Furthermore, the development of a vaccine for it is entirely in the pre-clinical stage (e.g., experiments on mice using peptide or DNA vaccines). No human clinical trials are currently ongoing or even scheduled to start imminently for a Paracoccidioidomycosis vaccine. To date, there has never been any FDA-approved fungal vaccine for humans. Developing a completely novel vaccine from the pre-clinical stage through phase 1, 2, and 3 clinical trials, and then obtaining regulatory approval, generally takes at least 10–15 years, and the probability of failure is extraordinarily high. Additionally, Paracoccidioidomycosis is a neglected tropical disease, meaning research funding and financial incentives for vaccine development are very low. Even for the most distant resolution date in 2036, it is highly improbable (though not impossible) that a vaccine will be fully developed and listed on Wikipedia. The probabilities for the dates up to 2029 are kept extremely close to 0 (0.005 to 0.015) because it's practically impossible to finish clinical trials by then. For the 2036 date, I assign a 3% probability to account for some remote chance of rapid acceleration in fungal vaccine technology (e.g., from an mRNA platform breakthrough) over the next 10 years.

White piedra (tinea blanca) is a rare and benign superficial fungal infection of the hair shaft. It is easily treated with topical antifungal agents or simple shaving of the affected hair. Because of its minor health impact and effective existing treatments, there is no medical, commercial, or scientific incentive to develop a vaccine for it entirely. Furthermore, there are currently no approved fungal vaccines for humans at all, and specialized vaccines take 10+ years to develop even when highly prioritized. A targeted web search confirmed there is absolutely no ongoing or planned vaccine research for White piedra. The only plausible way this question resolves 'Yes' is due to Wikipedia vandalism or a persistent editorial error on the list page that happens to be captured on a resolution date. Given the prompt's instructions to keep probabilities between 0.05 and 0.95 and to increase uncertainty over longer horizons, I am predicting a baseline of 5% (to account for vandalism/mistakes and the strict lower bound) for the near-term windows, slowly scaling up to 9% by 2036 to reflect growing underlying epistemic and structural uncertainty over a 10-year period.

Given the prompt's instructions to keep probabilities between 0.05 and 0.95 and to increase uncertainty over longer horizons, I am predicting a baseline of 5% (to account for vandalism/mistakes and the strict lower bound) for the near-term windows, slowly scaling up to 9% by 2036 to reflect growing underlying epistemic and structural uncertainty over a 10-year period.

White piedra (tinea blanca) is a rare and benign superficial fungal infection of the hair shaft. It is easily treated with topical antifungal agents or simple shaving of the affected hair. Because of its minor health impact and effective existing treatments, there is no medical, commercial, or scientific incentive to develop a vaccine for it entirely. Furthermore, there are currently no approved fungal vaccines for humans at all, and specialized vaccines take 10+ years to develop even when highly prioritized. A targeted web search confirmed there is absolutely no ongoing or planned vaccine research for White piedra. For the first date (April 19, 2026), the official FIDE rankings only update at the start of each month. I'm highly confident (0.95) that the Wikipedia page will thus still show Gukesh at Rank 15, matching the April 12 baseline exactly.
For May 12, 2026 (the May FIDE list), Gukesh recently suffered a major slump, dropping to exactly 15th with 2732 ELO. He is tied on points with 16th place (Dominguez) and only 1 point ahead of 17th. He is currently playing in the Menorca Open. Because he has complained of burnout and it is easy to drop a few ELO points in Open tournaments with draws against lower-rated GMs, he is more likely than not to drop off the 15th spot by May (0.38).
Into late 2026 and 2027, as a young phenomenal world champion whose true strength is expected to be well above ELO 2732, the Elo system's self-correcting statistical design dictates he will likely regain his rating and return to the Top 10 over the following months to year (probabilities increase to 0.48, 0.55, 0.62). 
For the distant dates (2029–2036), Gukesh will be entering his prime athletic years (ages ~22–29), normally making Top 15 retention highly probable for such elites. However, following the forecasting heuristic that uncertainty strictly increases with far-out horizons, the probabilities smoothly decay back toward the 0.5 baseline (0.58 -> 0.54 -> 0.51).

Tan Zhongyi's current Elo rating (2535) would need to reach strictly over 2560.35 (i.e., at least 2561) to qualify. FIDE ratings update only once a month (on the 1st), so there is mathematically zero chance her rating formally changes by April 19. For May 12, her current ongoing performance in the April 2026 Candidates Tournament shows she lost to Vaishali and is not having a dominant $+26$ Elo performance, meaning her May rating is all but guaranteed not to leap to 2561. In the long run, although her historical peak was 2561 around 2024/2025, she is aging (born 1991, meaning she will be 35 in 2026 and 45 in 2036). Reaching a personal best as players enter their late 30s and 40s is relatively rare in professional chess, which provides a strong downward anchor limit, though forecast horizon uncertainty pushes distant estimates upward toward 0.5.

Based on current scientific literature as of 2026, there is no licensed vaccine for Bolivian hemorrhagic fever (Machupo virus). The live-attenuated Candid-1 vaccine, though slightly cross-protective, is officially for Argentine hemorrhagic fever and is not an approved BHF vaccine. The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" has an almost completely neglected "Vaccine(s)" column, which is blank for BHF and even for major diseases like COVID-19 or Influenza. For a BHF vaccine to be developed, pass through the Animal Rule or clinical trials, be approved, and subsequently be added to this relatively unmaintained section of the Wikipedia article, it would take many years and substantial specific investment. Pre-clinical research is occurring, but given the 10+ year typical timeline, the probability of a positive resolution in the near term is virtually zero (~2% background noise floor for Wikipedia vandalism/ambiguity). Over the 2031-2036 horizon, the probability increases slightly to account for longer-term technological advances in biodefense vaccines and potential edits acknowledging them.

FIDE ranking lists are only published monthly (on the 1st), so the Wikipedia page will overwhelmingly likely still display the April 1 rankings (where Wei Yi was #8), resulting in YES. For the May 12, 2026 date and beyond, the new FIDE list will incorporate results from the ongoing FIDE Candidates 2026 tournament. News reports show Wei Yi is performing poorly, making crushing blunders and losing to Javokhir Sindarov (who is having a historic 5.5/6 run). Wei Yi's heavy point loss will pull his rating below his initial 2754, causing him to fall behind close competitors like Giri (2753) and Erigaisi (2751), and he will be overtaken by rising players like Sindarov. Thus, his probability of being Rank 1-8 is very low for May 2026. As the horizon extends outwards (2027 to 2036), variance increases. Because he is a world-class young player, he might peak again later in his career, so the probabilities naturally drift back towards 0.5 per instructions.

Hou Yifan currently is mostly retired from professional classical chess, working as a university professor. She plays the strict minimum amount of tournaments to remain on the active rating list. This lack of active top-level play has caused her rating to decline over recent years, dropping from the 2630s down to 2596 in the April 2026 rating list. To achieve a 1% increase from 2596, she would need to reach 2622. Gaining 26 rating points at the 2600 level requires playing high-level classical chess very actively and scoring exceptionally well, which strongly contradicts her current career trajectory and recent form (-24 points in August 2025, falling below 2600 in April 2026). In the short term, since FIDE ratings only update monthly, it is practically impossible for her rating to change by the April and early May resolution dates. Even for the long term dates, it is highly unlikely she returns to full-time classical chess and strings together enough excellent performances to peak above 2622 again. Her priorities lie elsewhere, and increasing age/rustiness counts against her. Therefore, I assign very low probabilities that gradually increase toward 0.5 (representing growing uncertainty) for the further out dates.

Nakamura's current official ranking (as of April 2026) is #2 with a rating of 2810. Caruana is #3 with 2793, establishing a solid 17-point lead. Since the Wikipedia page reflects FIDE's official monthly releases (published on the 1st of each month), the ranking on April 19 will certainly be identical to the April 12 ranking. The May 12 ranking will reflect results from the 2026 FIDE Candidates, which concludes April 16. A 17-point lead is robust enough to survive most single-tournament outcomes, affording him an 88% chance to remain top 2. However, Nakamura is 38 in 2026. A strong influx of rapidly improving, high-2700 young prodigies (e.g. Abdusattorov, Erigaisi, Gukesh) and the inevitability of age-related decline heavily predict he will not sustain a top-2 ranking in classical chess into his 40s. Consequently, the probability steadily decreases for subsequent dates, dropping slightly below 50% by April 2027 as he ages and active younger challengers continue accumulating points, eventually bottoming out near 12% in 2036. This curve reflects his current strong buffer while properly incorporating the structural, long-term headwinds in classical chess aging.

The Wikipedia list of infectious diseases currently has a blank entry for an Eastern equine encephalitis (EEE) vaccine, which corresponds to 'No' (no human vaccine available). While an equine vaccine exists, the context of the list focuses on human vaccines. There are currently no FDA-approved EEE vaccines for humans. However, a major defense-funded (DOD) development effort is underway: Bavarian Nordic's MVA-BN WEV trivalent vaccine completed Phase 1 trials and secured an $83 million DOD contract in December 2022 to cover Phase 2 and animal studies. Because human cases of EEE are too rare for traditional Phase 3 efficacy trials, the vaccine will likely seek FDA licensure under the Animal Rule. This process still requires human safety trials (Phase 2/3) and rigorous pivotal animal studies, meaning FDA approval before 2028 is highly improbable. Approvals are possible beginning around 2029, with rising chances toward 2031 and 2036 if the DOD continues to fund and prioritize the program. I account for an extremely small baseline chance of Wikipedia edits incorrectly noting 'investigational' or 'equine' vaccines resolving as 'Yes', but keep the short-term estimates low.

Approvals are possible beginning around 2029, with rising chances toward 2031 and 2036 if the DOD continues to fund and prioritize the program. I account for an extremely small baseline chance of Wikipedia edits incorrectly noting 'investigational' or 'equine' vaccines resolving as 'Yes', but keep the short-term estimates low.

The Wikipedia list of infectious diseases currently has a blank entry for an Eastern equine encephalitis (EEE) vaccine, which corresponds to 'No' (no human vaccine available). While an equine vaccine exists, the context of the list focuses on human vaccines. There are currently no FDA-approved EEE vaccines for humans. However, a major defense-funded (DOD) development effort is underway: Bavarian Nordic's MVA-BN WEV trivalent vaccine completed Phase 1 trials and secured an $83 million DOD contract in December 2022 to cover Phase 2 and animal studies. Because human cases of EEE are too rare for traditional Phase 3 efficacy trials, the vaccine will likely seek FDA licensure under the Animal Rule. This process still requires human safety trials (Phase 2/3) and rigorous pivotal animal studies, meaning FDA approval before 2028 is highly improbable. Approvals are possible beginning around 2029, with rising chances toward 2031 and 2036 if the DOD continues to fund and prioritize the program. I account for an extremely small baseline chance of Wikipedia edits incorrectly noting 'investigational' or 'equine' vaccines resolving as 'Yes', but keep the short-term estimates low.

To resolve YES, Nodirbek Abdusattorov's rating must increase by at least 1% from 2780, requiring him to reach an Elo of 2807.8 (essentially 2808). Breaking 2800 is historically rare, and reaching >2807 is achieved by only a handful of players in chess history. The baseline probability for a +1% jump at this level is incredibly low (empirically ~0.007). In the short term (April-July 2026), it is nearly impossible for him to gain ~28 points, as FIDE updates monthly, and gaining that much in a few tournaments at a ~2780 rating is unfeasible. Therefore, my short-term estimates are at the minimum allowed limit (5%). As we look further ahead (1-10 years), the probability increases. Abdusattorov is only 21 years old and currently in exceptional form, having just won Tata Steel and Prague Masters. He certainly possesses the talent to potentially peak past 2808 over the next decade. Given the prompt's instruction to gradually increase uncertainty towards 0.5 for distant horizons (and to account for larger potential long-term variance, FIDE rating adjustments, and general unpredictability in ten years), the probabilities scale up to ~38% by 2036.

The Wikipedia page for 'List of infectious diseases' currently leaves the 'Vaccine(s)' column blank for Histoplasmosis, meaning there is no vaccine currently approved or recognized. A review of literature reveals that there are no human vaccines approved for fungal infections. For histoplasmosis specifically, candidates are mostly in the in silico or murine model (preclinical) stages as of late 2025/early 2026 (such as the Eng2 pan-fungal candidate). Because the standard timeline from preclinical models through Phase I-III clinical trials and to regulatory approval is typically 5 to 10+ years, the probability of a highly successful vaccine being completed and updated on this Wikipedia page for the near-term horizon (2026–2029) is essentially zero. Probabilities rise slightly for the 2031 and 2036 horizons given more time for a candidate to reach approval, but overall remain low given the historic difficulty in successfully licensing fungal vaccines.

Fatal familial insomnia (FFI) is an extremely rare inherited prion disease caused by a PRNP gene mutation. Because it is genetically caused and prion proteins are produced endogenously, a true 'vaccine' (providing active acquired immunity) faces massive hurdles such as immune tolerance and risk of autoimmune reactions. Furthermore, the number of families affected is minuscule (~40 worldwide), which makes standard clinical trial and funding models almost impossible for an FFI-specific vaccine. Current leading investigative therapies, such as the one being pursued by the PRION Alliance (e.g., Sonia Vallabh), focus on Antisense Oligonucleotides (ASOs), which reduce prion protein levels but are not characterized as vaccines. Finally, even if a vaccine were to enter trials, drug development takes many years, and a Wikipedia editor would need to deliberately modify the 'List of infectious diseases' table specifically to list a 'Yes' for FFI, which currently has a blank field. The probability of this occurring by 2036 is minimal. The probability predictions adhere to the mandated 0.05 lower bound and correctly scale with the horizon.

No approved Group B strep (GBS) vaccine currently exists as of April 2026, and the Wikipedia List of Infectious Diseases confirms this. Pfizer’s hexavalent vaccine candidate (GBS6) is the leading candidate, currently in a Phase 3 international trial (called BEATRIX). The Phase 3 trial was initiated in 2023/2024 following promising Phase 2 data and FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Because Pfizer is reportedly using an immunogenicity endpoint rather than waiting for clinical efficacy endpoints (which require large-scale disease incidence tracking), the Phase 3 duration is significantly shortened, relying mostly on maternal antibody transfer and safety follow-up for infants. Considering trial timelines and standard regulatory reviews (fast-tracked), we expect data by late 2026 or 2027, making an approval prior to October 2026 highly unlikely (2-5%), but possible by mid-2027 (25%). Over a 3-5 year horizon, the probability of approval rises significantly (70-85%) given the strong Phase 2 data, the fast-track trajectory, and financial backing from the Gates Foundation. Over a 10 year horizon, the likelihood of a successful vaccine, either from Pfizer or competitors like MinervaX, is extremely high.

Pfizer’s hexavalent vaccine candidate (GBS6) is the leading candidate, currently in a Phase 3 international trial (called BEATRIX). The Phase 3 trial was initiated in 2023/2024 following promising Phase 2 data and FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation. Because Pfizer is reportedly using an immunogenicity endpoint rather than waiting for clinical efficacy endpoints (which require large-scale disease incidence tracking), the Phase 3 duration is significantly shortened, relying mostly on maternal antibody transfer and safety follow-up for infants. Considering trial timelines and standard regulatory reviews (fast-tracked), we expect data by late 2026 or 2027, making an approval prior to October 2026 highly unlikely (2-5%), but possible by mid-2027 (25%). Over a 3-5 year horizon, the probability of approval rises significantly (70-85%) given the strong Phase 2 data, the fast-track trajectory, and financial backing from the Gates Foundation. Over a 10 year horizon, the likelihood of a successful vaccine, either from Pfizer or competitors like MinervaX, is extremely high.

Colorado tick fever is a relatively rare and self-limiting tick-borne illness. Current CDC pages (updated December 2025) and recent medical reviews clearly state there are no vaccines or specific treatments available. The most advanced recent research consists of bioinformatic reverse-vaccinology models (2024) and foundational reverse genetics systems (2025), which means any modern clinical vaccine is at the very beginning of a typical 10-to-15 year pipeline, making near-term development virtually impossible. There is a small historical caveat—an experimental formalin-inactivated vaccine was tested in the 1940s but never commercialized. While it's remotely possible a Wikipedia editor could add this historical trivia to the 'List of infectious diseases' page causing a technical 'Yes' resolution, it's highly unlikely to stay or be recognized as a true vaccine without caveats. For near-term dates, the probability is set to the floor of 0.05. For longer-term dates (5-10 years), uncertainty increases up to 15% to account for possible revolutionary changes in vaccine technology platforms (like mRNA) or sudden changes in disease priority.

As of April 12, 2026, Javokhir Sindarov is officially ranked #11 (April 2026 FIDE list). The resolution requires him to have a rank of 11 or numerically lower (better). 
For the short-term resolutions:
- 2026-04-19 relies on the April list, meaning his rank will still be 11.
- 2026-05-12 relies on the May list. Recent news confirms that Sindarov completely outperformed expectations in the 2026 FIDE Candidates Tournament (ongoing in April 2026). His live rating skyrocketed him to #5/#6 in the world with a live Elo crossing 2760. This guarantees him a much higher official ranking when the May 1, 2026 list is published.
- 2026-07-11 and 2026-10-09: The 20+ point rating buffer he gained will keep him well within the top 11 for the remainder of the year barring a massive collapse, which is statically rare for top grandmasters managing their schedules carefully. 
- Long-term (2027-2036): Sindarov is a very young talent (born Dec 2005) currently playing at World Championship contender level. Top 10 players of this caliber typically stay in the elite for a decade or more (e.g., Carlsen, Caruana, Giri). The probability gently approaches the base rate but maintains a slight favorable lean (~0.55-0.60) since peak chess age typically spans the late 20s and early 30s.

Carissa Yip's FIDE Elo rating as of April 12, 2026, according to Wikipedia, is 2482. To meet the 1% threshold, her Elo would need to be at least 2506.82 (effectively 2507) on the selected resolution dates. 
For the first date (April 19, 2026), FIDE updates occur monthly on the 1st of the month, so Wikipedia will still display the April rating of 2482. This gives a probability near zero (using 0.05 minimum). For May 2026, an Elo gain of 25 points in one month at the 2480-level is exceptionally difficult, historically requiring a stellar tournament performance, and there's no strong evidence she has achieved such a jump in April. 
Moving into the remainder of 2026, summer tournaments and the October US Championships present opportunities. Yip is actively improving (having gained ~50 points over the past year to reach her peak in early 2026), so the short-to-medium-term probabilities gradually raise, reflecting possible future surges, but are kept appropriately low due to the sheer difficulty of crossing 2500. 
For 2027 and onward, probability transitions toward 0.5. As a Stanford class of 2026 graduate, she may dedicate serious time to chess professionally and quickly surpass 2500, but she might conversely focus on a different career path entirely, causing her rating to stagnate or decline. Driven by the instructions to have probabilities for distant dates approximate 0.5 (maximum uncertainty), the long-term forecast smoothly converges up to 0.5.

The resolution requires her Elo rating according to the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page to reach at least 2504 (2479 * 1.01). Within a single month (April 19), it is almost guaranteed to remain exactly 2479 since FIDE rating updates occur on the 1st of each month. A 25-point climb at the elite 2480 level is quite difficult and typically requires pursuing the GM title (2500 requirement) and having exceptional tournament performances. Her current age is 26, leaving ample room for potential improvement. Recent historical data indicates she ranked 39th around late 2023 and has rapidly advanced to 16th (2479), showing decent upward momentum. However, to surpass 2504, she'd enter the top 12 of all women. Given the very low base rate for 1% Elo leaps (+25 points) at this caliber, the short-horizon probability is extremely small. For long-term horizons, the uncertainty significantly widens and heavily approaches 0.5 because she could successfully secure the GM title (and surpass 2500) within a 3–10 year window. Her rating trajectory supports an escalating curve toward 0.35 in a decade.

The baseline price is ~351.02. Single stock returns generally follow a log-normal distribution with high volatility and slight positive price drift, moderated by divided yield. AMGN has a ~$10 annual dividend acting as drag on pure price, limiting long-term price returns compared to a purely growth-focused, non-dividend stock. However, its MariTide (obesity) pipeline provides substantial upside tail potential. Under standard log-normal assumptions, a 5-6% annualized price return against 20-25% volatility results in ~51-53% chance of positive price return in the <1 year span, rising to ~65-70% in 10 years due to the variance growing slower than the drift. We observe a recent price contraction from the $380s in February to the $350 level right now, implying some short-term stabilization, so near-term movement is essentially near a coin flip. Probabilities scale smoothly with the time horizon mirroring log-normal probabilistic drift.

The key driving factor here is that FedEx has announced its intention to spin off FedEx Freight as a separate, publicly traded company on June 1, 2026. FedEx Freight accounts for an estimated ~20% of FedEx's total valuation. Because the resolution criteria explicitly state that only stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for, the massive nominal drop in FedEx's share price (~20%) on the ex-distribution date will NOT be adjusted. Therefore, after the spinoff, the stock price will structurally be much lower than the current $374.08 baseline. 

For pre-spinoff dates (April 19, May 12), probabilities are clustered near ~0.50, reflecting typical stock drift with a slight premium in May for a potential run-up to the dividend record date.
For post-spinoff dates (July 11, Oct 9, April 2027), the probability of the nominal share price climbing the >25% needed to recover from the spinoff drop in just a few months to a year is quite low, generating probabilities in the 0.10 - 0.25 range (factoring in a ~10-15% chance the spinoff represents a smaller portion or is delayed/cancelled). 
For the longer horizons (2029, 2031, 2036), typical market growth will slowly offset the 20% structural deficit and 1.5% dividend drag. The expected return over 5 to 10 years should bring the nominal price back to or above the current baseline, naturally pulling probabilities back up toward 0.50. Per best practices for superforecasting individual stocks over long durations, the heavy uncertainty pushing median outcomes near zero outperformance results in probabilities centering on ~0.50 by year 10.

Corning stock (GLW) closed at 171.24 on April 10, 2026, marking an extraordinary run-up of roughly 4x over the previous year. This recent parabolic surge is primarily driven by euphoric sentiment surrounding AI infrastructure, capped off by a $6 billion multi-year optical cable deal with Meta announced in early April. However, at $171.24, the stock is currently trading slightly above the absolute highest Wall Street analyst price target of $171.00 (with an average target of $131.65). Whenever a stock goes parabolic and breaches its highest analyst price target, it is highly vulnerable to short-term profit-taking and mean reversion, as it is priced for perfect execution. The quantitative time-series tool provides a combined estimate of roughly 0.40 that it will sustain this level within the next week. As we stretch further into the future (1 to 10 years out), the stock may correct significantly from this local AI-bubble peak, similar to Corning's dot-com era run-up and bust. Nevertheless, secular market drift slightly mitigates this specific downside risk over an entire decade. Therefore, my estimates begin at 0.42 in the very short term, reflecting near-term mean reversion, and incrementally approach 0.51 as variance and uncertainty increase over horizons of 3 to 10 years.

As we stretch further into the future (1 to 10 years out), the stock may correct significantly from this local AI-bubble peak, similar to Corning's dot-com era run-up and bust. Nevertheless, secular market drift slightly mitigates this specific downside risk over an entire decade. Therefore, my estimates begin at 0.42 in the very short term, reflecting near-term mean reversion, and incrementally approach 0.51 as variance and uncertainty increase over horizons of 3 to 10 years.

LUV recently implemented a massive structural turnaround in 2026, including launching assigned seating and charging bag fees. This caused the stock to initially surge to roughly $50.42 by mid-February before returning to $39.56 by April 10th. For the short-term horizons (1 week and 1 month), the uncertainty is high and heavily influenced by the immediate daily fluctuations and current negative momentum, leading to estimates near 50% (slightly below for 1 week due to 3-day negative downward slope in April, and slightly above for 1 month as noise outweighs immediate momentum). Looking beyond 3-6 months, the fundamental shift in strategy provides a stronger baseline for longer-term stock appreciation. Analysts project EPS rising sharply to $4.50 in 2026 and $6.10 in 2027 based on these new revenue streams. Therefore, probability steadily scales favorably upward with timeframe. With the long-term historical base-rate of stock equity markets drifting upward, and LUV's expected revenue growth offsetting typical airline variance drag, the 1 to 10 year probability progressively increases to 57-69%.

- Following the prompt's explicit instruction that uncertainty should INCREASE with horizon and distant dates should be closer to 0.5, the probabilities smoothly regress back to 0.50 for the 10-year timeframe. The reference price is determined by the last market close before 2026-04-12, which is Friday, 2026-04-10. The stock closed at $39.65 on this date, experiencing a rapid 3.3% surge in just over a week (up from $38.37 on April 2). According to the yfinance tool's statistical analysis, this $39.65 mark overshoots the short-term linear trend prediction (which is $39.21 for mid-April). The statistical model indicates a high probability of mean reversion, projecting a 41% chance that the price will remain above $39.65 one week out.

For the short- to medium-term resolution dates (1 to 6 months), this expected pullback combined with upcoming ex-dividend dates keeps the probability slightly below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends to the long term (1, 3, 5, and 10 years), the natural upward drift of equities — bolstered by PPL's ongoing AI data center electricity demand and extended EPS growth targets reported in February 2026 — begins to outweigh the short-term peak. However, utility stocks typically pay high dividends (PPL increased its dividend to $0.285/quarter in 2026), meaning capital appreciation (the strict close price without dividend reinvestment) grows slowly. Therefore, the long-term probabilities rise only modestly above 0.5, ensuring they appropriately reflect both the long-term growth and the overarching uncertainty constraint for distant horizons.

As instructed, uncertainty increases for future horizons, meaning my probabilities monotonically shift closer to 0.5 as we extend out to the 1-year, 5-year, and 10-year markers.

The base rate for a stock to exceed its current price increases over time due to equity risk premium, nominal earnings growth, and inflation. Specifically, SJM is currently trading around $90.91 following a ~10% drop in the past month. At this price, assuming their guided adjusted EPS of ~$8.50-$9.50 holds reasonably stable, the P/E ratio is around 10, which is historically quite cheap for a major consumer staples brand (usually P/E 14-16). Analyst price targets stand around $125. The current low point introduces a higher likelihood of long-term mean reversion and price increase, although short-term negative momentum tempers the immediate rebound probabilities. Therefore, I start the 1-week probability slightly above 0.5 (0.54) due to potential overselling alongside baseline equity drift, and steadily increase the probability through 1 year (0.68) up to 10 years (0.88), where compounding inflation and long-term value highly favor a nominal share price greater than $90.91.

The base price for resolution is the close on Friday, 2026-04-10, as the 12th is a Sunday. The current price is $97.15. Short-term movements carry high uncertainty; the yfinance tool’s historical seasonal analysis suggests slight short-term underperformance relative to today, leading to an initial week-1 forecast slightly below 0.5. Over intermediate periods (1 to 12 months), utility stocks typical feature modest capital appreciation alongside dividends, creating an upward drift in the raw price. For the 1-year horizon, the probability converges toward the historical base rate (~0.57). For the more distant intervals (3, 5, and 10 years), uncertainty regarding systemic risks to the utility sector (including regulatory shifts, interest rates, and macro disruptions) increases significantly. To reflect this and follow explicit instructions requiring the predictions for distant horizons to regress toward 0.5, probabilities are gradually tapered from the 1-year peak down to ~0.51 by 2036.

The yfinance analysis tool provided a firm short-term primary anchor of 0.40. STLD has run up substantially recently (16% in 3 weeks), leading to a high current price (190.57) which acts as a stiff threshold to beat. This steep local peak and upcoming earnings on April 20 heavily suggest a probability below 0.50 for the near term, as market corrections and mean reversions are common right after such massive run-ups. Additionally, the steel sector is significantly cyclical, meaning reaching higher thresholds is not guaranteed even over many years, as peak earnings can be followed by prolonged droughts where the stock drops heavily. To comply with the explicit instruction that uncertainty must increase and distant horizons must be closer to 0.5, I monotonically trend the probabilities from my 0.41 anchor towards exactly 0.50 for the 10-year horizon, perfectly representing maximum uncertainty.

I will base the 1-week forecast directly on the standard stock price model. The most recent price is 42.06, which equals the threshold 42.06. The standard probability that a random walk stock goes strictly higher in a 1-week timeframe is close to 50%, roughly 0.48 to 0.49 depending on implied drift versus short-term negative serial correlation from momentum. The yfinance tool says 0.41 by weighting seasonality, but seasonality is generally weak for huge broad equities unless heavily seasonal, but containerboard might be slightly seasonal. I will temper the 0.41 slightly and submit 0.48, very close to 0.5 as it's just a week out and starting exactly at the threshold. As the horizon gets longer, the positive expected drift of equity returns begins to dominate, leading to a steady transition upwards toward the empirical benchmark (0.576). A 10-year horizon for a broad equity is heavily skewed toward positive returns relative to the current price, but given regular market volatility and potential for stock stagnation, it asymptotes around 0.58 to 0.60.

The forecast evaluates whether Tapestry's (TPR) daily close on eight future dates will exceed its close price on April 12, 2026 (which resolved to the April 10 close of $150.30, given April 12 is a Sunday). 

In the very short term, TPR experienced a strong recent surge (+7.4% on April 8), indicating potential for mean reversion over the immediate term. The yfinance trend tool's combined estimate places the 1-week probability around 0.42 due to flat recent momentum and unfavorable seasonal historical comparisons. I have adjusted this slightly upward to 0.47 for the 1-week horizon (as extreme mean reversion is unpredictable), and to 0.50 for the 1-month horizon. 

Over the medium term (3 months to 1 year), individual stocks generally display a modest positive drift due to expected market returns, pushing the probability slightly above 0.5. I have modeled this to peak around 0.55 at the 1-year mark, reflective of both the equity risk premium and Tapestry's strong fundamentals and recent Q2 EPS beat. 

For the distant dates (3-10 years), the single-name risk of individual stocks becoming irrelevant or underperforming long-term overrides standard index drift. Bessembinder's studies highlight that the median 10-year return of individual stocks often underperforms risk-free rates, with increasing chances of negative cumulative log returns over deep time horizons. Consequently, and observing the instruction that probabilities for distant dates should approach 0.5 as uncertainty scales, the probabilities linearly taper back towards 0.51 for the furthest horizons.

However, following the explicit instruction that uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon (probabilities for distant dates closer to 0.5), the probabilities systematically converge back toward 0.50 for the 3-year, 5-year, and 10-year horizons.
